channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Four Individuals Deported Back to India After Being Forced into Bangladesh
item.description: Four individuals from West Bengal's Birbhum district, who were allegedly forced into Bangladesh in June 2025, were finally brought back to India after a prolonged legal battle. The group, including Su...

item.title: EU's Digital Border System (EES) Faces Operational Challenges at Airports
item.description: The European Union's Digital Border System (EES), designed to streamline cross-border travel and enhance security, has encountered significant operational challenges at airports across member states. ...

item.title: Government Payroll Scandal Under Investigation: DCI Probes KSh 6.2 Billion Fraud
item.description: The Directorate of Criminal Investigations (DCI) has launched a probe into a suspected KSh 6.2 billion payroll fraud after receiving a special audit report highlighting widespread irregularities in go...

item.title: Tanzania's 50th Dar es Salaam Trade Fair Aims for Global Hub Status by 2050
item.description: The 50th Dar es Salaam International Trade Fair (DITF) 2026 officially opened on July 3, 2026, with President Samia Suluhu Hassan and Mozambique’s President Daniel Chapo inaugurating the event. The fa...

item.title: Poll suggests broad Canadian support for most Alberta referendum proposals, though experts question the wording
item.description: A new Leger poll commissioned by the Association for Canadian Studies suggests that many Canadians, both inside and outside Alberta, support most of the province's upcoming referendum questions that f...

item.title: US Consulates in Lahore and Karachi to Resume Consular Services After Security Review
item.description: The US Embassy in Islamabad announced that consulates in Lahore and Karachi will restart regular consular services from July 20, 2026. This follows a suspension since March 2026 after violent protests...

item.title: A beginner's guide to getting started with parkrun in the UK
item.description: Parkrun, a global 5km running event held weekly in parks, is accessible to all fitness levels. The article details practical advice for newcomers, emphasizing that participation doesn't require athlet...

item.title: 21bet.ng Enhances Matchday Experience for Crypto-Savvy Football Fans in Nigeria
item.description: The entry of 21bet.ng into Nigeria's market is set to transform how crypto-savvy football fans engage during matchdays. With a focus on merging sports betting with cryptocurrency payments, the platfor...

item.title: Medusa cable project aims to boost African internet infrastructure
item.description: The Medusa undersea fibre-optic cable project, designed to connect Europe and Africa, addresses critical gaps in Africa's telecommunications network. This initiative, part of broader efforts like Equi...

item.title: Heavy Rain in Uttarakhand Causes Landslides, Blocks 71 Roads, Woman Killed
item.description: Severe heavy rains battered Uttarakhand on Wednesday, triggering landslides that blocked 71 roads, killed a woman in Pithoragarh district, and created a 600-metre-long artificial lake on the Ramganga ...

item.title: Bernie Sanders, Graham y Platner anuncian retiro de la campaña
item.description: El senador Bernie Sanders, el representante Jim Gray y el exgobernador de Nueva York Andrew Cuomo han anunciado su retiro de la carrera presidencial de 2024. La decisión de Sanders, quien había sido u...

item.title: Global Advocacy for Nuclear Disarmament and Radiation-Free Initiatives
item.description: The article highlights a 22,000-mile journey undertaken to promote nuclear disarmament and advocate for a radiation-free environment. It emphasizes the urgency of global efforts to eliminate nuclear w...

item.title: US Conducts Strikes on Iran in Response to Tanker Attacks
item.description: The US military launched strikes against Iran's central command following Iranian attacks on American military sites in the Gulf. President Donald Trump announced the strikes during a NATO summit in A...

item.title: US-Iran conflict escalates as military strikes resume in Hormuz
item.description: President Donald Trump declared the U.S.-Iran ceasefire invalid, vowing to retaliate against Tehran's actions in the Strait of Hormuz. The strategic waterway remains a critical flashpoint after Irania...

item.title: South Africa Study Reveals Toxic Chemicals in Children's Products via Recycling
item.description: A University of Cape Town (UCT) study has uncovered traces of banned toxic chemicals in various children's products sold in South Africa, highlighting concerns about contamination through recycled mat...

item.title: BBC director general calls for reform of TV licence fee system
item.description: The BBC's new director general, Matt Brittin, has criticized the traditional TV licence fee model as outdated and unsustainable. In a speech to MPs, he described the current system as 'a busted flush'...

item.title: Carney backs U.S. response to Iranian attacks as NATO leaders debate escalating Middle East tensions
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney said Iran acted irresponsibly after attacks on commercial shipping and regional targets during what had been a period of de-escalation following the death of Iran's Supreme ...

item.title: Replacing Sugary Drinks with Water and Whole Fruit Reduces Hypertension Risk
item.description: A study published in *Circulation* found that high intake of sugar-sweetened beverages (SSBs) and fruit juice is associated with an increased risk of hypertension. Researchers analyzed data from 25,74...

item.title: African Leaders Urge Cultural Unity at Sukuma Festival
item.description: National Liberal Party Leader Dr. Augustus Kyalo Muli emphasized the importance of cultural heritage in fostering African unity during the Sukuma 7.7 Cultural Festival in Tanzania. The event, organize...

item.title: Impeachment trial concludes first week with contextual clips and competency debates
item.description: The first week of the impeachment trial concluded with a focus on contextual clips, clarifications, and discussions about the competence of those involved. The proceedings highlighted key moments from...
